LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT IMPROPERLY RELIES ON THE DECLARATION OF COUNSEL AND AN UNAUTHENTICATED CONTRACT THAT IS NOT PART OF THE COMPLAINT TO SUPPORT ITS ARGUMENT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR #11185; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

NOTICE OF APPEARANCE (TRANSACTION ID # 57901900) FILED BY DEFENDANT THE SALVATION ARMY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT THE SALVATION ARMY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE RULING THAT GRANTED THIS MOTION AS UNOPPOSED ON SEPTEMBER 9, 2015, IS VACATED. THAT RULING WAS MADE IN ERROR. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. "[N]O PUBLIC POLICY OPPOSES PRIVATE, VOLUNTARY TRANSACTIONS IN WHICH ONE PARTY, FOR A CONSIDERATION, AGREES TO SHOULDER A RISK WHICH THE LAW WOULD OTHERWISE HAVE PLACED UPON THE OTHER PARTY." (TUNKL V. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (1963) 60 CAL.3D 92, 101.) THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT, HOWEVER, WENT ON TO EXPLAIN THAT WHERE THE SUBJECT MATTER OF A CONTRACT AFFECTS THE PUBLIC INTEREST, THE RELATIVE BARGAINING POSITIONS OF THE PARTIES ARE NOT THE SAME AS IN A PRIVATE, VOLUNTARY TRANSACTION. "CALIFORNIA COURTS HAVE INVALIDATED RELEASES OF LIABILITY FOR FUTURE ORDINARY NEGLIGENCE . . . WHEN . . . THE COURT DETERMINES THAT A PARTICULAR RELEASE CONCERNS A SERVICE THAT TRANSCENDS A PURELY PRIVATE AGREEMENT AND AFFECTS THE PUBLIC INTEREST." (CITY OF SANTA BARBARA V. SUPERIOR COURT (2007) 41 CAL.4TH 747, 757.) EXCULPATORY CLAUSES THAT OFFEND THE PUBLIC INTEREST ARE ARGUABLY INVALID. (TUNKL V. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (1963) 60 CAL.3D 92DEFENDANT HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN UNDER AGUILAR. DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON DICTA FROM OPINIONS INVOLVING RECREATIONAL SPORTS IS UNPERSUASIVE. WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S WORKPLACE INJURY WAS BASED ON PASSIVE OR ORDINARY NEGLIGENCE, OR WHETHER THE SCOPE OF DEFENDANT'S AGREEMENT SPECIFICALLY WARNED PLAINTIFF OF A KNOWN RISK. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =(302/AJR)

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-03-2015 Text Ruling

Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-29-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant The Salvation Army's Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication - The ruling that granted this motion as unopposed on September 9, 2015, is vacated. That ruling was made in error. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. "[N]o public policy opposes private, voluntary transactions in which one party, for a consideration, agrees to shoulder a risk which the law would otherwise have placed upon the other party." (Tunkl v. Regents of the University of California (1963) 60 Cal.3d 92, 101.) The California Supreme Court, however, went on to explain that where the subject matter of a contract affects the public interest, the relative bargaining positions of the parties are not the same as in a private, voluntary transaction. "California courts have invalidated releases of liability for future ordinary negligence . . . when . . . the court determines that a particular release concerns a service that transcends a purely private agreement and affects the public interest." (City of Santa Barbara v. Superior Court (2007) 41 Cal.4th 747, 757.) Exculpatory clauses that offend the public interest are arguably invalid. (Tunkl v. Regents of the University of California (1963) 60 Cal.3d 92.) Defendant argues that The Salvation army does not provide a service of great importance to the public. Defendant nonetheless concedes that drug rehabilitation of convicted prisoners, which Defendant provides, is important in our society. Defendant likewise concedes that the County of Contra Costa County refers individuals to volunteer at The Salvation Army because of overcrowded county jails. "There is a triable issue of material fact if, and only if, the evidence would allow a reasonable trier of fact to find the underlying fact in favor of the party opposing the motion in accordance with the applicable standard of proof." (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) Defendant has not met its burden under Aguilar. Defendant's reliance on dicta from opinions involving recreational sports is unpersuasive. Whether Plaintiff's workplace injury was based on passive or ordinary negligence, or whether the scope of Defendant's agreement specifically warned Plaintiff of a known risk. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =(302/AJR)
